methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Venus Aerospace, a Series B-stage developer of supersonic and hypersonic propulsion systems founded by Sassie and Andrew Duggleby, operates within a narrow sector comprising two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two material private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus). The company has raised $44M to date.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, yields a valuation of $84M. This approach applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier—typical for Series B advancement—to the $44M capital raised proxy, reflecting both the equity deployed and anticipated value creation through the funding round. Peer-median analysis could not be applied: while two same-sector private companies exist, they do not meet the threshold of three comparable datapoints required for robust median calculation across stage and valuation-tier filters. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce material noise and was therefore excluded.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $71M (low) to $96M (high), with $84M as the median estimate. This range reflects typical early-stage venture step-up dynamics. Key uncertainties include the absence of a sufficient peer cohort in this nascent sector, which limits external validation of the multiplier applied, and lack of disclosed financial or technical milestones from Venus Aerospace itself. A higher-conviction valuation would require either additional private comparable data or disclosure of Venus Aerospace's development stage and commercial progress metrics.
